Verifying whether that inferential chain holds (premises, intermediates, conclusion) is a plausible primary task given any substantive 'look'.","R1.3":"Concrete empirical claims are asserted (DPSR rankings diverge from RMSE rankings; lowest-RMSE emulator ranks third on DPSR, attributed to specific process failures) without inline evidence tables or citations. Checking whether stated results are grounded in the described experiment is a plausible primary task.","R1.4":"An explicit priority claim is made ('to our knowledge, CLIMATE-RUBRIC is the first benchmark to decompose...'); assessing whether this claim survives comparison to nearest prior work in process-based NWP evaluation and existing climate emulator benchmarks is a plausible primary task."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on (a) writing quality and prose clarity — sentence structure, precision of language, narrative flow — or (b) scientific and logical soundness — specifically whether the 'first benchmark' novelty claim holds against prior work, whether the empirical DPSR-vs-RMSE results are adequately evidenced, and whether the argument that DPSR supersedes aggregate scoring is logically tight?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact's core contribution rests on a causal argument — that DPSR exposes per-process failures that aggregate RMSE masks. Verifying whether that inferential chain holds (premises, intermediates, conclusion) is a plausible primary task given any substantive 'look'.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete empirical claims are asserted (DPSR rankings diverge from RMSE rankings; lowest-RMSE emulator ranks third on DPSR, attributed to specific process failures) without inline evidence tables or citations. Checking whether stated results are grounded in the described experiment is a plausible primary task.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit priority claim is made ('to our knowledge, CLIMATE-RUBRIC is the first benchmark to decompose...'); assessing whether this claim survives comparison to nearest prior work in process-based NWP evaluation and existing climate emulator benchmarks is a plausible primary task.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on (a) writing quality and prose clarity — sentence structure, precision of language, narrative flow — or (b) scientific and logical soundness — specifically whether the 'first benchmark' novelty claim holds against prior work, whether the empirical DPSR-vs-RMSE results are adequately evidenced, and whether the argument that DPSR supersedes aggregate scoring is logically tight?
